expectations.text: Advises against changing things while progress is still occurring
expectations.evidence: 'When you have a program that's working reasonably well, the instinct to switch is often premature.' 'Don't switch because of vague dissatisfaction.' 'Most programs work for 6-12 months if you tweak them intelligently.'

expectations.text: When plateau appears, recommends deciding whether the lift needs more stress or less stress
expectations.evidence: Section 3 covers both adding stimulus (add sets, increase frequency, add supplemental exercises) and reducing stress (reduce intensity temporarily, add rest days, deload strategically).

expectations.text: Recommends small, targeted changes (volume, intensity, frequency, or exercise selection) evaluated individually over weeks
expectations.evidence: 'Make one or two changes at a time, not five. Give each tweak 3-4 weeks to show results.' Covers volume, exercise selection, intensity, frequency, and progression as individual levers.

expectations.text: Only switch programs entirely after serial targeted tweaks have failed
expectations.evidence: 'Switch programs only if: You've made 2-3 targeted tweaks over 8-12 weeks with no progress.' Multiple other conditions listed, all requiring exhausting tweaks first.
